title: Rootful: v3.4 CNI_DELETE not called on dnsname plugin
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

kind bug

**Description**

In v3.4.7 patch https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/31df5b78fcdf3a492ef063eb1b98a3b4715e5969 does not seem to work.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
1. # podman run -d --rm --name web1 --network net1 docker.io/library/busybox:latest sleep 1
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Entry for web1 remains in /run/user/0/containers/cni/dnsname/net1/addnhosts after container ends.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Entry removed, and if last container on net1 then dnsmasq for net1 stopped and /run/user/0/containers/cni/dnsname/net1/ cleaned up.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

It seems this should be fixed from 3.4.4 with https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/31df5b78fcdf3a492ef063eb1b98a3b4715e5969

But it seems the cni plugins are called from vendor/github.com/containernetworking/cni/pkg/invoke/raw_exec.go which is not patched.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:      3.4.7
API Version:  3.4.7
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Mon Aug  8 12:00:00 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes - 4.3.0-dev works. Dnsname puts its files are under /run/containers/cni/dnsname/

comments.body:
We no longer support v3.4.7 upstream, also it is expected that the commit you link does not work in v3.X since this is part of the new v4.0 network backend code which is unused on v3.
Please use v4 or as workaround unset XDG_RUNTIME_DIR in your root session.
